title: GitHub Actions supply-chain risk â€” third-party Action ingestion, SHA-pinning, pwn-request, cache poisoning
description: A platform engineer adopts third-party Actions in CI workflows. Recent incidents â€” trivy-action March 2026 (75/76 tags compromised), tj-actions/changed-files (~23k repos affected), Sysdig Shai-Hulund worm (November 2025), Wiz prt-scan campaign â€” have all exploited the same primitive: workflows referencing Actions by mutable tag rather than commit SHA, and orgs without an allow-list policy. Hulumi's `OrgFoundation` (M2) ships the SHA-pin-required Actions allowlist policy at startup-hardened tier; M3's `HulumiGithubHardeningPack` enforces this declaratively at preview-time.
actors: Platform Engineer (trusted, configures the org-level allowlist)
Workflow Author (semi-trusted, picks Actions from the allow-listed set)
Compromised Maintainer of a third-party Action (untrusted, primary attacker)
AI Coding Agent (trusted, authoring workflows; could be tricked into citing mutable tags)
assets: Workflow YAML files referencing third-party Actions
Repository secrets accessible to the workflow
Build artifacts produced by the workflow

stride.name: Third-party Action repo compromise (trivy-action / tj-actions pattern)
stride.description: The maintainer's account is compromised (phishing, credential stuffing, or a separate supply-chain reach); attacker pushes a malicious commit to the Action's source repo and re-tags `v1` to point at it. Workflows referencing the Action by mutable tag pick up the malicious version on the next run. Mitigation: SHA-pin every external Action; org-level `ActionsOrganizationPermissions` policy with `shaPinningRequired: true` at startup-hardened tier.

stride.name: Cache poisoning via Action with cache-write capability
stride.description: A compromised Action with `actions/cache` write access poisons the cache for a subsequent legitimate run. The legitimate run consumes the poisoned cache and ships compromised artifacts. Mitigation: cache scoping by branch + workflow; scope-limit Actions with cache-write to the minimum branch set.

stride.name: Pwn-request via PR diff content reaching write context
stride.description: A pull-request workflow runs against attacker-controlled PR diff content with `pull_request_target` (which carries the base-repo's secret context). Attacker scripts in the diff exfiltrate secrets to an attacker-controlled endpoint. Mitigation: minimize secret access on PR workflows; ensure `permissions: contents: read` default at workflow level (`OrganizationSettings.defaultWorkflowPermissions`).

stride.name: Information disclosure via runner exfil after Action compromise
stride.description: A compromised Action running on the org's runner reads environment, secrets, or filesystem state and exfils to an attacker-controlled URL. Step-Security Maintained Actions or octo-sts-style short-lived token exchange limit blast radius.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/baseline.github.OrgFoundation
recommendedComponents.rationale: Provisions the org-level `ActionsOrganizationPermissions` with `shaPinningRequired: true` at startup-hardened tier; configures the Actions allowlist (selectedActionsPatterns).

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/policies.github.HulumiGithubHardeningPack
recommendedComponents.rationale: H1 declaratively rejects raw `github.Repository` not wrapped in `SecureRepository`; H2/H3/H4 enforce ruleset, OIDC, and tier-monotonicity invariants.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/drift.adapters.GithubWebhookFallbackAdapter
recommendedComponents.rationale: Detects org-level allowlist drift via the `repository_ruleset` and `organization` webhook events; surfaces `tier-degraded` honestly for non-GHEC tiers.
